decision_summary: Constraints are satisfied with a significant margin (58.6°C vs 85.0°C limit). Per optimization history, geometry changes are hitting legality limits and improvement has stalled. The strategy shifts to material tuning as the primary lever. I will increase the heat spreader conductivity by the maximum allowed factor (2.0x) to enhance lateral heat spreading and further reduce the peak chip temperature.

changes.reason: Maximizing spreader conductivity within the allowed 2.0x step ratio to improve lateral heat distribution and lower chip peak temperature, as geometry adjustments are currently constrained.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
Improved thermal coupling between the chip and the cooled boundaries
risk_notes: Diminishing returns: doubling conductivity may yield smaller temperature drops than previous iterations due to bottleneck shifting to base material or boundary conditions.
Material property of 1000 W/(m*K) exceeds typical engineering materials (e.g., diamond ~2000, copper ~400), representing an idealized scenario.
